title: Document approval workflow for regulatory submissions
trigger: A document is submitted for approval via the review portal
success_outcome: The document progresses through draft, review, and approved states with an audit trail
tldr: Multi-step document approval: draft → under-review → approved with audit trail

in: State transitions: draft → under-review → approved or rejected

behavior: Step 1: author submits document (draft → under-review). Step 2: reviewer approves or rejects. Approved documents move to approved state; rejected documents return to draft. Each transition appends an audit log entry with actor and timestamp.
business_rules:
- DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND: reject operations on unrecognised document ids with 404.
- INVALID_TRANSITION: only permitted state transitions are allowed; reject others with 409 INVALID_TRANSITION.
- Reviewers may not approve their own submissions; return SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN (403) otherwise.
acceptance_scenarios: Given an author with a draft document, when they submit it, then the document moves to under-review and an audit entry is created
Given a reviewer, when they approve an under-review document, then status becomes approved
Given the original author acting as reviewer, when they try to approve their own document, then 403 SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN is returned
inputs_outputs_errors: Input: documentId (UUID), action (submit|approve|reject), reviewerId (UUID). Output: updated Document with new status and audit trail. Errors: 404 DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND, 409 INVALID_TRANSITION, 403 SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN.
definition_of_done: All state transitions are covered by tests
Audit trail is written for every transition
